question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to outline his plans for the future provision of services at Daisy Hill Hospital.
answer_text: I wish to clearly state at the outset that Daisy Hill Hospital provides a vital service to the local population and is an important part of NI’s current and future hospital network. Changes over recent months include:Temporary Divert of Daisy Hill Stroke Services to CAH site Emergency General Surgery consolidated on the Craigavon Area Hospital siteSustainable general medical inpatient services following implementation of a Stabilisation Plan for the Daisy Hill Hospital siteThe Stabilisation Plan has responded to risks identified with the overall aim to enhance Same Day Emergency Care (SDEC) and community-based approaches. Key actions have included:12 consultants now in place and supporting a new medical model with support from senior consultants and a full complement of junior doctors.	Improvements in patient flow with private ambulance provision doubled, control room enhancement, a discharge lounge in place, an 8-Bed Short Stay Unit has also been developed as well as an enhanced Ambulatory Unit.		There have been other key developments including infrastructure on the Daisy Hill site also and have included; an MRI modular unit and continued development of the regional Elective Overnight Stay Centre (EOSC). 	My Department and the Trust remains committed to the future development of Daisy Hill Hospital site as an integral part of the hospital network and to ensure safe services for the Newry population.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will undertake a safety review of the A28 road junctions at Markethill and Gosford Forest Park.
answer_text: My Department has undertaken a review of the injury collision history at the road junctions and access to Gosford Forest Park. Two junctions met the threshold in our policy to be investigated further.  These further investigations confirmed that, regrettably, none of the injury collisions had a causation that would be mitigated by engineering measures.My Department will continue to monitor the junctions and if there are any significant changes then a further review will be undertaken using the latest PSNI injury collision data.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for his assessment of road safety on the main A3 Portadown to Armagh road, following a recent spate of road traffic accidents.
answer_text: Officials have reviewed the injury collision information provided by the PSNI along the A3 Portadown to Armagh Road.  Along this stretch of road, the majority of collisions have occurred at road junctions and accesses.  However, there are no common causation factors identified that could be resolved through further safety engineering measures.  The Department therefore has no plans to implement any further road safety interventions on this section of the A3 at this time.  However, the Department will continue to monitor the injury collision information to identify if further safety measures are required.In the meantime, my Department is progressing the A3 Portadown Road resurfacing and widening scheme at the climbing lane section out of Armagh which, on completion, will improve safety on this section of road by improving the road surface and providing right turning lanes for the road junction and accesses.One of the best ways to ensure the safety of our roads and fulfil our long term goal of eliminating death and serious injury on roads by 2050 is by changing driver behaviour.  If all of us who are drivers do not change our attitudes towards speeding and careless driving, our death toll, which has increased in the past year, will continue to increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will consider the installation of a 20mph speed limit to serve Derryhale Primary School.
answer_text: Part-time 20mph limits near schools are implemented based on assessments considering collision history, speed, traffic volume, infrastructure, and site constraints.  Schools have been assessed in accordance with DfI’s policy ‘Road Safety at Schools’ which can be viewed at: https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/road-safety-schools-rsppg-e070The program to date has been rolled out in two tranches, with the most recent completed in November 2022.  Over the coming weeks, I will be considering my priorities, including the potential for a further tranche of the 20mph schools programme, in the context of the funding that is currently available to my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will install a crash barrier to enhance safety at the bend on Portadown Road, Armagh.
answer_text: This location has been assessed but does not meet the assessment criteria for further engineering safety measures, taking into account the latest injury collision data from the PSNI. The Department is not therefor proposing a crash barrier scheme at this stage.However, the current resurfacing and widening road scheme at this location aims to improve the safety along  this section of carriageway by laying a new road surface as well as providing right turn lanes which will benefit the users of this road including local residents.Concerns regarding driver behaviour such as speeding, or parking which causes an obstruction to other road (and footway) users should be directed to the police using the non-emergency number 101.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will introduce a lower speed limit to aid motorist safety on (i) Mullaghbrack Road; (ii) Gosford Forest Park; (iii) Main Street; (iv), Newry Street; and (v) Tandragee Road junctions of the A28.
answer_text: The Department’s policy for setting the appropriate speed limit for a road aims to ensure speed limits are consistent and motorists are presented with the same speed limit on similar roads taking into account factors such as: the characteristic of the road; the speed of existing traffic; the degree of frontage development; and road traffic collision data, which is continuously monitored using data provided by the PSNI. The current speed limit on the A28 Gosford Road is in line with our current policy document and considered to be appropriate.My Department has previously applied road safety engineering measures at the four road junctions by providing right turning lanes which allow vehicles turning right to wait safely before making their manoeuvres. The safety at these junctions have further been supported through the provision of advance directional signs including for Gosford Forest Park.One of the best ways to ensure the safety of our roads and fulfil our long term goal of eliminating death and serious injury on roads by 2050 is by changing driver behaviour. If all of us who are drivers do not change our attitudes towards speeding and careless driving, our death toll, which has increased in the past year, will continue to increase. Concerns regarding driver behaviour such as speeding should be directed to the police using the non-emergency number 101.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on plans to introduce legislation to extend the validity period of Ukrainian driving licences in Northern Ireland from 12 months to 36 months.
answer_text: A public consultation on extending the validity period of Ukrainian driving licences in Northern Ireland from 12 months to 36 months ran from 6 September 2023 to 3 October 2023. A total of 137 responses were received and overall highlighted strong levels of support for the proposal. A synopsis of results was published on the DfI website on 23 January 2024. The associated amending legislation, the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2024 (S.R. 2024 No. 22), was made on 27th February 2024 and laid before the NI Assembly on 29 February 2024. It is intended that the Rule will come into operation on 25th March 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on resurfacing plans for (i) Cloghanramer Road; and (ii) the Shandon Park area of Newry city.
answer_text: Resurfacing schemes are funded through my Department’s Structural Maintenance Programme; regrettably, for some time the available investment has not kept pace with what is needed to maintain all of our roads in a reasonable condition.  While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention. I am very aware of the long-term underfunding from which our road network has suffered. Therefore, I have recently announced the immediate investment of an additional £1m in road maintenance to begin to address the growing backlog of pothole repairs. This much needed funding will be used between now and the end of the financial year to target areas of particular need with small scale resurfacing schemes designed to repair areas that have suffered the most and where localised repairs are less effective. I have also allocated over £8m of additional funding to structural maintenance to allow further large scale resurfacing schemes to be delivered. This represents a significant investment in improving the structural integrity of our road network.Details of the planned list of resurfacing schemes for the 2024/25 financial year in the Newry, Mourne and Down council area will be included in the Department’s Annual Report to Council in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will commit to necessary road surface improvements along the Tandragee 100 motorcycle road race course in order to permit racing to proceed in 2025.
answer_text: While I appreciate the significance of this event, regrettably I am unable to commit our limited road funding to undertake works which are not necessary for everyday road use especially when compared to the many other areas in need of investment. The Department currently has no plans to undertake road improvements at this time.However, following engagement between my officials and race organisers in December 2023 it was agreed that the Department would carry out a design and cost estimate for the improvement works that would allow the race organisers to consider if they could find the funds necessary for my Department to take forward the procurement of the work on their behalf.  The cost estimate work is currently ongoing and on completion officials will engage further with the race organisers.In the meantime routine inspections and repairs along the A51 Armagh Road will continue in accordance with the current maintenance standards.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the replacement of the electric vehicle rapid charger at Fiveways, Newry.
answer_text: The current electric vehicle public charge point network is owned by the Electricity Supply Board (ESB) and is operated on a commercial basis. ESB announced in November 2021 that it had been awarded £3.27 million from the Levelling Up Fund to expand and enhance the EV charging network in the North. ESB is best placed to provide an update on the replacement of the electric vehicle rapid charger at Fiveways, Newry. ESB can be contacted directly at ecars@esb.ie or enquiries can be sent directly to ESB, Two Gateway, East Wall Road, DUBLIN 3, D03 A995. The market is also open to other commercial operators who would wish to provide charging infrastructure. My Department has also set up an EV Infrastructure Task-Force which brings together representatives from government, consumers, energy providers, industry and EV drivers to consider our EV Infrastructure requirements and to help set out a clear action plan by autumn 2022 to deliver a fit for purpose, modern EV charging network.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the Health and Social Care Special Recognition Payment for staff in the independent sector.
answer_text: Phase 2 had been briefly paused to allow time to seek clarity on a number of issues raised relating to the impact of the increase in national insurance contributions which are included as part of the payment provided to the former employer.  A further letter was issued to employers on 14 April 2022 providing clarity on the issues raised and confirming recommencement of the payment process. Claims are being processed by BSO in date order when all relevant information is provided by the claimants. No date has been set for the closure of the scheme and claims will continue to be processed by the Business Services Organisation during 2022/23. I can confirm that as at 1 June 2022, a total of over £16.65m has been paid out under Phase 1 to independent sector care home and domiciliary care providers covering 28,457 individuals. As at the same date over £3.56m has been paid out to 9,496 individuals under Phase 2; these payments include staff previously employed in independent sector care home and domiciliary care providers who have since moved employer or left the sector, and staff employed with eligible day opportunity providers and buildings-based day services.In addition we have also opened the Scheme to eligible applicants in the following groups, Personal Assistants employed by Independent Living Fund recipients, Thalidomide Health Grant recipients, and Personal Assistants employed by those in receipt of Direct Payments from HSC Trusts. This element of the scheme was launched on 23 March and is being delivered by ILF Scotland for the payments, Scotland Excel who set up and run the online portal, and SDS Scotland who manage the dedicated helpline. These delivery partners were heavily involved in delivering the equivalent scheme for the Scottish Government.  To date £419k has been paid. To encourage uptake on this element Trusts have now written to all individuals in receipt of direct payments. My officials are also finalising arrangements to include eligible staff whose employers are no longer trading and I hope to be in a position to include this cohort of individuals in the coming weeks.A FAQ on the Special Recognition Payment has been published on the Department’s website:- https://www.health-ni.gov.uk/news/hsc-staff-recognition-payment-faqsThis continues to be updated with relevant information as each phase of the claims process progresses. I remain personally committed to ensuring the recognition payment continues to be paid to independent sector staff as quickly as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether there is grass cutting equipment available for use by staff within each DFI Roads depot.
answer_text: The Department’s grass cutting programme is delivered through a combination of our internal staff resource and external contractors. Grass cutting equipment is available in each depot as required.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what action has been taken to restore safe footpath access for mobility scooter users on the A27 Portadown to Tandragee road.
answer_text: As you may be aware, this work is scheduled to be undertaken by a developer and I can confirm that my officials have been engaging with the developer of this site in order to have the necessary works undertaken.  I am pleased to advise that the developer has confirmed that the necessary works will be completed with the next 4 to 8 weeks.
